prompt:
Write a Python function unfold_headers(lines: list[str]) -> list[tuple[str, str]] that parses email/HTTP-style header lines (each list element is one physical line WITHOUT newline characters) into an ordered list of (name, value) tuples.

Rules:
1. A physical line that begins with a space or a tab is a CONTINUATION of the previous logical header line. If the very first line is a continuation, raise ValueError.
2. Every logical header line must contain a colon. The name is everything before the FIRST colon; the raw value is everything after it. A logical line with no colon (including an empty line "") raises ValueError.
3. The name must be non-empty and consist only of ASCII letters, digits, and the hyphen '-'. Anything else (spaces before the colon, underscores, an empty name) raises ValueError.
4. Names are case-insensitive: lowercase the name in the output. Duplicate names are allowed and must ALL be kept, in input order.
5. Value assembly: strip leading and trailing whitespace from the after-colon text; strip each continuation line's content likewise; join all the non-empty pieces with a single space. A header whose pieces are all empty has value "" (e.g. the single line "A:" gives ("a", "")).
6. A continuation line whose content is entirely whitespace raises ValueError.
7. The input list must NOT be mutated.
8. An empty input list returns [].

Output ONLY the function definition.
